response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile engineer with nearly 20 years in software, but their profile centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/full-stack work rather than the deep, current C++ expertise this role requires. Their only professional C++ experience was a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015, and there is no evidence of MQTT, industrial protocols, IEC 62443/SDL practices, or industrial automation domain experience. While they have relevant adjacent strengths (low-latency systems, event-driven architecture, testing culture), the core skill mismatch is too large for a senior C++ role in industrial automation.
strengths: Strong systems background with low-latency, high-throughput services (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Solid engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
Event-driven and distributed-systems experience via GISCollective and microservices work
concerns: Minimal and dated professional C++ experience (~7 months in 2014–2015); primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
No industrial cybersecurity exposure — no IEC 62443, SDL, or secure-development lifecycle evidence
No MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, or industrial automation/IoT protocol experience listed

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443 background
